questions.explanation: {tex} \because \int\limits_0^5 {(1 + f(x))dx }{/tex} = 7 {tex} \therefore \int\limits_0^5 {dx + \int\limits_0^5 {f(x)dx } } {/tex} = 7 {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ x \right]_0^5 + \int\limits_0^5 {f(x)dx} {/tex} = 7 {tex} \Rightarrow \int\limits_0^5 {f(x)dx} {/tex} = 7 - 5 = 2, Also, {tex}\int\limits_{ - 2}^5 {f(x)dx} {/tex} = 4 {tex} \Rightarrow \int\limits_{ - 2}^0 {f(x)dx} + \int\limits_0^5 {f(x)dx} {/tex} = 4 {tex}\Rightarrow\int\limits_{ - 2}^0 {f(x)dx}{/tex} = 2

{tex}I = \int\limits_0^{\frac \pi 2} {\frac{1}{{1 + \tan x}}dx}{/tex} {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac \pi 2} {\frac{{\cos x}}{{\cos x + \sin x}}dx}{/tex} ....(1) Then, {tex} I = \int\limits_0^{\frac \pi 2} {\frac{{\cos (\frac{\pi }{2} - x)}}{{\cos (\frac{\pi }{2} - x) + sin(\frac{\pi }{2} - x)}}} \ dx {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_0^{\frac \pi 2} {\frac{{\sin x}}{{\sin x + \cos x}}dx}{/tex} ...(2) adding 1 and 2, we get... {tex} 2I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}dx = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}

questions.question_html: {tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /2} {\frac{1}{{1 + \sin x}}} dx{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation: {tex} I = \int\limits_0^{\pi /2} {\frac{1}{{1 + \sin x}}dx}{/tex} {tex} \\ = \int\limits_0^{\pi /2} {\frac{{1 - \sin x}}{{1 - {{\sin }^2}x}}} dx {/tex} ....(multiply num. and deno. by [1 - sinx ]) {tex}= \int\limits_0^{\pi /2} {\frac{{1 - \sin x}}{{{{\cos }^2}x}}} dx {/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\tan x - \sec x} \right]_0^{\pi /2}{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\frac{{\sin x - 1}}{{\cos x}}} \right]_0^{\pi /2} {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\frac{{ - \cos x}}{{1 + \sin x}}} \right]_0^{\pi /2}{/tex}

{tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /2} {\log \left| {\cos x} \right|} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_0^{\pi /2} {\log \left| {\cos (\frac{\pi }{2} - x)} \right|} dx {/tex} {tex} = \int\limits_0^{\pi /2} {\log \left| {\sin x} \right|dx} {/tex} {tex} = - \frac{\pi }{2}\;\log 2{/tex} (standard result)

questions.question_html: {tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /2} {\log } {/tex} (tan x) dx is equal to
questions.explanation: {tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /2} {\log (\tan x)dx} = \int\limits_0^{\pi /2} {\log (\tan (\frac{\pi }{2} - x)} )dx.\implies\int\limits_0^{\pi /2} {\log (\cot x)\ dx}{/tex}

questions.question_html: {tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /2} {\log } {/tex}(cot x) dx is equal to
questions.explanation: {tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /2} {\log (\cot x)dx} {/tex} {tex} I= \int\limits_0^{\pi /2} {\left\{ {\log (\cos x) - \log (\sin x)} \right\}dx} \;\;\dots \dots(1){/tex} {tex} I=\int\limits_0^{\pi /2} {\left\{ {\log (\sin x) - \log (\cos x)}\right\}dx}\;\;\;\dots\dots(2){/tex} Adding (1) and (2) {tex}2I=\int\limits_0^{\pi /2} 0.\ dx \implies 0{/tex}
f(x) = x|x| is an odd function as : {tex}f( - x) = ( - x)\left| { - x} \right|= - x\left| x \right| = - f(x){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\int\limits_{ - 2}^2 {x\left| x \right|dx} = 0{/tex}
Subsitute tan -1 x = t then {tex}\frac{1}{1+x^2}dx=dt{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\int t\;dt = \frac{t^2}{2}+C \Rightarrow \frac{({tan^{-1}x})^{2}}{2}+C{/tex}

questions.question_text: The value of the integral {tex}\int\limits_0^1 {{e^{{x^2}}}} {/tex} dx lies in
questions.question_html: The value of the integral {tex}\int\limits_0^1 {{e^{{x^2}}}} {/tex}dx lies in
questions.explanation: {tex}0 \leqslant x \leqslant 1 \Rightarrow 0 \leqslant {x^2} \leqslant 1{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \int\limits_0^1 {1dx \leqslant \int\limits_0^1 {{e^{{x^2}}}dx \leqslant \int\limits_0^1{edx }}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 1 \leqslant \int\limits_0^1 {{e^{{x^2}}}dx \leqslant e} {/tex}

questions.question_text: {tex}\int\limits_0^2 {\sqrt {\frac{{2 + x}}{{2 - x}}} dx} {/tex} is equal to
questions.question_html: {tex}\int\limits_0^2 {\sqrt {\frac{{2 + x}}{{2 - x}}} dx} {/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation: {tex}\int\limits_0^2 {\sqrt {\frac{{2 + x}}{{2 - x}}dx} } {/tex} {tex} = \int\limits_0^2 {\frac{{2 + x}}{{\sqrt {4 - {x^2}} }}} dx{/tex} {tex} = 2\int\limits_0^2 {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {4 - {x^2}} }}} dx + \int\limits_0^2 {\frac{{xdx}}{{\sqrt {4 - {x^2}} }}} {/tex} {tex} = 2\left[ {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}(\frac{x}{2})} \right]_0^2 - \frac{1}{2}.\int\limits_0^2 {\frac{{ - 2x}}{{\sqrt {4 - {x^2}} }}} dx{/tex} {tex} \\ = 2(\frac{\pi }{2}) - \frac{1}{2}\left[ {\frac{{\sqrt {4 - {x^2}} }}{{1/2}}} \right]_0^2{/tex} {tex}= \pi - (0 - 2) = \pi + 2{/tex}

questions.question_html: {tex}\int {\tan x} {\ sec ^2x}{/tex} dx is not equal to
questions.explanation: Let tanx = t, then sec 2 xdx = dt {tex} \implies \frac{{{t^2}}}{2}+C \implies \frac{{ta{n^2}x}}{2}+C = \frac{1}{2}{(\cos x)^{ - 2}}+C'{/tex}
